title: fraxinus nigra
intro: Fraxinus nigra, also known as black ash, is a deciduous tree native to North America, characterized by its dark, grayish-black bark and compound leaves. The bark and leaves of the black ash have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of black ash include relieving pain and reducing fever, as well as treating various skin conditions and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of black ash include teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by infusing the bark and leaves in hot water or a solvent, or by applying the infused oil to the skin.
